summary: On March 14, 2026, Ukrainian kamikaze drones struck the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai, causing a massive fire confirmed by video and NASA FIRMS data. The "Rubikon" Center reported 136 enemy targets destroyed in operations across Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman. Russian forces used FPV drones to destroy Ukrainian fixed-wing "Hornet" UAVs and enemy unmanned boats near Tendrovskaya Spit. Ukrainian troops employed FPV drones in both offensive strikes—such as destroying a camouflaged Russian tank—and tactical maneuvers, including targeting infantry and even attempting to dismount Russian soldiers from horses. A Russian infantryman was filmed charging an FPV drone with a bayonet and losing the engagement. Ukrainian FPV pilots are using advanced models capable of striking targets over 100 km behind Russian lines, while Russian forces struggle to replicate the technology.
